source.description: Six-panel built-in image generation contact sheet saved under drafts/cartoon-coconut-drink/ and cropped into raster tutorial assets.
source.notes: Current Doodlea.day lesson uses a June 26 coconut hook without claiming public activity or popularity.

process_strategy: A single six-panel marker contact sheet keeps one coconut drink centered while each frame adds the coconut body, top opening, face, straw, umbrella, marker fills, and a final edge-and-color pass.

final_elements.name: round coconut body
final_elements.description: A big round coconut shape with a slightly flattened base.

final_elements.name: top opening and rim
final_elements.description: An oval opening at the top with a dark drink rim.

final_elements.name: cute face
final_elements.description: Two simple eyes and a small smiling mouth on the coconut.

final_elements.name: bendy straw
final_elements.description: A striped bendy straw leaning out of the top opening.

final_elements.name: tiny paper umbrella
final_elements.description: A small triangular drink umbrella tucked behind the straw.

final_elements.name: brown and tropical marker fills
final_elements.description: Brown coconut fill with bright straw and umbrella color accents.

frames.visible_job: Blocks in the round coconut body and flattened base.
Adds the oval top opening and dark rim shape.
Draws the simple eyes and small smile on the coconut.
Places the bendy straw and tiny drink umbrella at the top.
Adds brown coconut color and bright straw and umbrella fills.

final_step.visible_job: Retraces the established coconut rim, straw, umbrella, and face while deepening only the existing marker fills.
allowed_changes: small highlight cleanup on established elements

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
